fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Storage Area; Landscaped Area; Perimeter Fencing
fr.considerations: Limited Side Access; Multiple Structures; Limited Rear Access; Fenced Sections
fr.recommendations: Ensuring that side and rear access points are sufficiently wide and unobstructed could improve emergency responder movement around the property. The presence of perimeter fencing might limit access at certain points, so installing clearly marked gates or access points could facilitate easier navigation for emergency personnel. Consideration could be given to maintaining open pathways between the multiple structures on the property to enhance movement and operational flexibility. Reserving clear zones within the vehicle parking area may support effective responder staging and provide additional space for emergency vehicles. Attention to landscaping around these areas may further enhance visibility for responders navigating the property during critical situations.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Between Structures
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space could be achieved by increasing vegetation separation around the structures and reducing the concentration of vegetation adjacent to the fence and structures. Creating more open areas around the buildings may help decrease fuel continuity and improve wildfire resilience. Managing vegetation found between structures could further decrease the likelihood of fire spreading across the property. Expanding clear space around the structures and maintaining cleared zones around fences could support suppression efforts. Improving this separation and continuity management might also facilitate firefighter access during suppression operations. By maintaining and expanding defensible space, fuel reduction around structures could be achieved, lending to a more prepared property that is better aligned with long-term resilience goals.
